data.name: Text_NoMonitorsDiscovered
data.comment: Text-mode output when list/get finds no monitors.

data.name: Text_NotSupported
data.comment: Text-mode marker for a setting the monitor does not support.

data.name: Text_Unknown
data.comment: Text-mode marker for a supported setting whose value was not read.

data.name: Text_Failed
data.comment: Text-mode marker that an apply-profile setting failed at the hardware.

data.name: Text_NotConnectedSkipped
data.comment: apply-profile: a monitor named by the profile is not currently connected.

data.name: Text_NoSettingsInProfile
data.comment: apply-profile: the profile entry for a connected monitor had no values.

data.name: Text_OutOfRangeSkipped
data.comment: apply-profile: a profile value was outside the valid range and was not written.

data.name: Text_NoProfilesSaved
data.comment: profiles command: no saved profiles exist.

data.name: Text_NoValuesReported
data.comment: capabilities: a discrete VCP code advertised no enumerated values.

data.name: Text_AppliedProfile
data.comment: {0} = profile name.

data.name: Error_SelectorMissing
data.comment: The flag names (--monitor-number, -n, --monitor-id, -i) are CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_MonitorNotFoundById
data.comment: {0} = monitor id.

data.name: Error_MonitorNotFoundByNumber
data.comment: {0} = monitor number.

data.name: Warn_MonitorNumberIgnored
data.value: warning: --monitor-number {0} ignored because --monitor-id was also provided
data.comment: {0} = monitor number. Flag names are CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Hint_RunList
data.value: run 'powerdisplay list' to see available monitors
data.comment: 'powerdisplay list' is a literal command and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_DiscreteParse
data.value: --{0} value '{1}' could not be parsed as a name or hex value
data.comment: {0} = setting name (CLI syntax, e.g. input-source), {1} = the value the user passed.

data.name: Error_DiscreteUnsupported
data.value: --{0} value '{1}' is not in the monitor's supported set
data.comment: {0} = setting name (CLI syntax), {1} = the value the user passed.

data.name: Hint_DiscreteValue
data.value: pass a name from the supported list above, or a raw hex value like 0x11
data.comment: 0x11 is a literal hex example and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_Orientation
data.value: --orientation value '{0}' is not accepted; expected one of {1}
data.comment: {0} = the value the user passed, {1} = comma-separated accepted degrees (0, 90, 180, 270).

data.name: Hint_Orientation
data.value: pass the rotation in degrees (0, 90, 180, or 270)

data.name: Error_OutOfRange
data.value: --{0} value {1} is out of range; expected integer in [{2}, {3}]
data.comment: {0} = setting name (CLI syntax), {1} = the value, {2} = min, {3} = max.

data.name: Error_NoSettingSpecified
data.value: no setting specified; pass one of --brightness/--contrast/--volume/--color-temperature/--input-source/--power-state/--orientation
data.comment: The flag names are CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_OnlyOneSetting
data.value: only one setting may be applied per 'set' call
data.comment: 'set' is a literal command name and must not be translated.

data.name: Hint_OnlyOneSetting
data.value: split into multiple invocations: one --<setting> per call

data.name: Error_UnsupportedAdjustment
data.comment: {0} = monitor number, {1} = monitor name, {2} = setting name (CLI syntax, e.g. contrast).

data.name: Hint_UnsupportedReason
data.comment: {0} = a technical reason string.

data.name: Error_ConfirmPowerOff
data.value: refusing to power down or sleep Monitor {0} ({1}) without confirmation
data.comment: {0} = monitor number, {1} = monitor name.

data.name: Hint_ConfirmPowerOff
data.value: re-run with --confirm-power-off to power the display off or put it to sleep
data.comment: --confirm-power-off is CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_OrientationNoGdi
data.value: Monitor {0} ({1}) does not have a GDI device name and cannot be rotated via Windows display settings
data.comment: {0} = monitor number, {1} = monitor name.

data.name: Error_HardwareWriteFailed
data.comment: Fallback message when the driver returned no detail.

data.name: Error_UnknownSetting
data.comment: {0} = the setting name the user passed to --setting.

data.name: Hint_ValidSettings
data.comment: {0} = comma-separated canonical setting names (CLI syntax, not translated).

data.name: Error_ProfileNotFound
data.comment: {0} = profile name.

data.name: Hint_RunProfiles
data.value: run 'powerdisplay profiles' to see available profiles
data.comment: 'powerdisplay profiles' is a literal command and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_TimedOut
data.comment: {0} = number of seconds.

data.name: Error_UnexpectedError
data.comment: {0} = exception message.

data.name: Error_NegativeTimeout
data.value: --timeout must be >= 0 (use 0 to disable the timeout).
data.comment: --timeout is CLI syntax and must not be translated.
